content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814


March 14, 1977

Ms. Janice Madigan
Treasurer, SDCPRW 1976
432 E Street
San Diego, California 92101

Dear Ms. Madigan:

We are in receipt of the copy of your letter of December 31,
1976 to Mrs. Goldie Joseph concerning the repayment to her of
surplus funds.

There is no provision of the Political Reform Act which would
prohibit the reimbursement or repayment of money forwarded to
a committee established pursuant to the law. All transactions
must, of course, be reported by the committees involved.

Very truly yours,

REGINA SICILIANO-KUTCHINS
Technical Assistance Program Coordinator


RSK:mak

TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE/ADMINISTRATION (916) 322 5660 • EXECUTIVE/LEGAL 322 5901 • ENFORCEMENT CONFLICT OF INTEREST 322 5641

F P P C
[stamp] JAN 29 1977

San Diego County Federation of Republican Women's Clubs
432 E STREET                    SAN DIEGO, CALIFORNIA 92101 •              TELEPHONE (714) 232-1063

PRESIDENT
MRS. HARALSON SMITH
641 ALAMEDA BLVD.
CORONADO, CA 92118

1ST V. PRESIDENT
MRS. RICHARD DOWD
1011 FUERTE DRIVE
LA MESA, CA 92041

2ND V. PRESIDENT
MRS. GEORGE DANIELS
14250 CAROLYN COURT
POWAY, CA 92064

3RD V. PRESIDENT
MRS. GEORGE R. PIPER
2437 NEWPORT AVE.
SAN DIEGO, CA 92107

RECORDING SECRETARY
MRS. ALAN COMSTOCK
1823 COWLEY AVE.
SAN DIEGO, CA 92103

CORRESPONDING SECRETARY
MRS. FRANK LIEURANCE
6685 WAHL ST.
SANTEE, CA 92071

TREASURER
MRS. JANICE MADIGAN
3030 CURLEY ST.
SAN DIEGO, CA 92103

AUDITOR
MRS. JAMES FLOYD
1373 EL REY AVE.
EL CAJON, CA 92021

December 31, 1976


Mrs. Goldie Joseph
515 Via Lido Soud
Newport Beach, California 92660

Dear Mrs. Joseph:

I appreciate the opportunity to have spoken to you about
the remaining funds from the check which you sent me for the
postage for the initiative drive to repeal Proposition Twenty.

I understand that it is your position that the money did
not come from a committee established under the guidelines of
the Fair Political Practices Commission (FPPC). Rather the
money was collected informally and was forwarded to the San
Diego County Federation of Republican Women (SDCFRW) from
your personal checking account. Unfortunately, the money was
already accepted by SDCFRW with the understanding that you
were a committee organized under the guidelines established
by the FPPC.

Therefore, I cannot return the money to you until the
FPPC has ruled on this matter. By copy of this letter, I am
notifying the FPPC of this matter and am asking for their
assistance in the resolution of the issue.

The SDCFRW will most assuredly apprize you of all
further developments. Thank you for your patience and
understanding.

Sincerely,

[signature]
Janice Madigan
Treasurer, SDCFRW 1976

cc: FPPC
Helen Smith, President SDCFRW
Etta Mae Fisher, Incoming Treasurer, SDCFRW
